open_issues.note: Cores avg 12-47 LH @11min (humans 60-80). Engine CS is best available (OHA custom micro measured WORSE, C3 -3.6k). Real gains need proper attack-timing CS micro. Biggest econ lever.
Teams collect only ~43% of spawned lane gold; crude role-split caps measured neutral (C10/B1 reverted). Needs real lane-assignment soak logic (who rotates to which empty lane).
DK 218gpm/12LH, PA 228, SB 239, slardar 249 vs sniper 431 @10min — per-hero early-game rework is the big tail. Matches the project's focus-hero polish direction.

Owner observation (live watching iter-0004): bots' fight willingness seems weak and push tempo slower than human games — no proactive 'take tower after won fight' linkage, no dedicated push strategy. Long-term per-hero/macro polish item, not a blocker for this stage.
Pre-fix losing side did ~0 building dmg. If this persists with fair drafts (post iter-0001 cohort), hunt a defend-lock in mode desires beyond the fixed gates.
All games flag GetDesire hotspots, max ~7ms (sniper/CM/lion/PA/slardar...). Frame-budget risk; profile after macro fixes settle.
~74/game engine rejections: orders on dead units (order 19/20). Sloppy target validation, low priority.
Pre-fix feeder/low_gpm stats (lich 55K/207D etc.) all confounded by rigged draft. Re-rank from post-iter-0002 games.

CLOSED-as-explained: engine error handler is broken in this env — ALL Lua error text is masked ('error in error handling'), print() never reaches logs, so log-based unmasking is impossible. Debugging real errors needs in-game observation or bisection. Was open_issues:vscript_errors:masked.

iter-0001.note: remote tag push blocked by env git proxy; tag exists on farm checkout + locally. Farm loops restarted for version-stamp provenance.

iter-0006.note: tower-aggro yo-yo fix + 5-min turbo early game (4c723d1). Same run prefix soak/run_20260719_0741; cohort split by script_version stamp (iter-0005 / iter-0005-2-g* / iter-0006).

run collector ~07:05 UTC after ~10 slot1 replays; sandwiched_walk cand<base = win; watch kill participation
confirm Venge overextend drops in next batch; revert if it kills Venge fight participation

open_questions: SPOT RECLAMATION at ~07:00: both validation/replay spots gone (0 instances, no leak). Re-run: depthnum (Class-A --validate), nodive2 (behavioral), nopush/suplh/warlock queued.
Lesson: spot reclamation interrupts time-sensitive validation; --validate self-terminate handles cost but not reclaim. Consider on-demand for short validations or retry-on-reclaim.

workflow.loop: observe frame-by-frame -> find concrete bad decision -> narrow gated fix -> replay-fixture local validation (mandatory) -> accumulate ~10 -> ONE batch A/B final gate
rules: simulator is NEVER the per-change validator (lanefix bundle straight-to-sim was rejected 0/4; per-fix isolation batches were killed as waste)
fixtures: make_fixture.py + tests/mock/replay_fixture.lua run REAL jmz helpers on REAL frames, no J.* stubs
autonomous mode is owner-toggled; heartbeat = background sleep (in-memory cron dies on suspend)
